pr.title: ft: improve Crazy Hand init command dispatch
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Closed, unmerged PR improving Crazy Hand's human command dispatcher function ftCh_Init_80156AD8. The diff replaces a btn_pressed inline helper with a local buttons macro plus cached L-button test, swaps several A/Z command mappings, replaces inline 0 and 2 float literals with ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060 externs, and exposes those externs in ftCh_Init.h. The PR body reports checkdiff improvement to 98.99317% and a successful configure+ninja build, but explicitly says the function remains unmatched with remaining pad-word register allocation drift. A later issue comment says the work was already done elsewhere.

key_files.role: Main implementation change for ftCh_Init_80156AD8 command dispatch, button-mask checks, A/Z mapping, and float constant uses.
evidence_refs: changed_files: ftCh_Init.c added 35 deleted 37 hunk 1
raw_diff: ftCh_Init_80156AD8 hunk
pr_body: improves ftCh_Init_80156AD8 and describes button mapping, L-button caching, named zero/two float constants

key_files.role: Header declaration surface for ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060 extern float constants.
evidence_refs: changed_files: ftCh_Init.h added 3 deleted 0 hunk 1
raw_diff: added extern f32 ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060

what_changed.text: Removed static inline btn_pressed(u32 mask) and introduced a local buttons macro for HSD_PadMasterStatus[3].button, scoped around ftCh_Init_80156AD8 and undefined afterward.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removal of btn_pressed and addition of #define buttons/#undef buttons

what_changed.text: Cached the L-button mask test in u32 l_pressed and reused it for the L + D-pad command branches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u32 l_pressed = buttons & HSD_PAD_L
pr_body: caching the L-button check in the same style as the matched Master Hand dispatcher

what_changed.text: Changed Crazy Hand dispatcher button mapping so BackAirplane2/BackAirplane3 use Z + D-pad up/right, while ftCh_Init_801597F0 and ftCh_Init_80159F40 branches use A + D-pad directions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_PAD_A branches changed to HSD_PAD_Z for BackAirplane2/3
raw_diff: HSD_PAD_Z branches changed to HSD_PAD_A for ftCh_Init_801597F0 and ftCh_Init_80159F40
pr_body: fixing the human command dispatcher button mapping

what_changed.text: Replaced repeated pos.z = 0 assignments with pos.z = ftCh_Init_804DA05C and replaced random range multiplier 2 with ftCh_Init_804DA060.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pos.z replacements with ftCh_Init_804DA05C
raw_diff: da->x90/x94 random expressions use ftCh_Init_804DA060
pr_body: naming the zero/two float constants used by Vec3 setup and random range math

what_changed.text: Added header extern declarations for ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCh_Init.h extern f32 additions

smart_moves.text: The PR preserves an explicit remaining-drift note instead of claiming a match, identifying pad-word register allocation and derived mask temporaries as the remaining mismatch area.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Known remaining drift paragraph

smart_moves.text: The author reports narrow checkdiff for the touched symbol and a full configure+ninja build, which makes the claimed score improvement auditable within the PR evidence.
evidence_refs: pr_body: checkdiff command and configure.py && ninja OK
bot_report: ftCh_Init_80156AD8 before 98.86% after 98.99%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When cleaning a command dispatcher, verify button mapping against the intended command table or sibling dispatcher before treating decompiler-style helper calls as correct.
scope: Crazy Hand/Master Hand-style human command dispatch
evidence_refs: pr_body: fixing the human command dispatcher button mapping
raw_diff: A and Z branch mappings swapped

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a repeated modifier-button condition, caching the shared mask result can be a matching tactic when local sibling code suggests that shape, but the PR evidence only supports this for the L-button check in ftCh_Init_80156AD8.
evidence_refs: pr_body: caching the L-button check in the same style as the matched Master Hand dispatcher
raw_diff: l_pressed reused across L + D-pad branches

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not claim byte match from a score improvement alone; record the exact remaining mismatch area when a function remains unmatched.
evidence_refs: pr_body: checkdiff -> 98.99317%
pr_body: function still not a byte match and remaining drift described

naming_conventions.text: The PR uses address-style names ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060 for float constants rather than semantic names, consistent with conservative naming when only address/data identity is shown.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060 uses
raw_diff: ftCh_Init.h extern declarations
decomp_standards: conservative-naming

naming_conventions.text: Local variables use short role names fp, da, pos, rand, and l_pressed; the newly introduced l_pressed directly names the cached L-button mask result.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Fighter* fp, ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs* da, Vec3 pos, float rand, u32 l_pressed

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace a small inline helper that reloads a pad word with direct macro-based access and a cached mask temp for one repeated condition.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: btn_pressed removal and buttons/l_pressed addition
pr_body: remaining drift isolated to full pad-word register allocation and derived mask temp registers
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The function still did not match; this should not be generalized without local sibling and objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use named address-style float data for repeated zero/two constants when the PR scope and diff tie those constants to Vec3 setup and random range math.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pos.z = ftCh_Init_804DA05C
raw_diff: random range multiplier ftCh_Init_804DA060
pr_body: naming the zero/two float constants
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Current standards warn against promoting literals to named storage without ownership evidence; this PR slice does not include section/symbol ownership proof beyond the author claim and diff.

notes: The diff and PR body strongly support what changed in ftCh_Init_80156AD8 and ftCh_Init.h.
Verification is self-reported in the PR body plus bot score report; no independent build logs or objdiff output are loaded.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so this intake must not treat the changes as accepted or landed.
The author references matched Master Hand dispatcher style, but that sibling source is not included in the loaded PR slice.
Float constant naming is visible in the diff, but section ownership or symbol metadata evidence for those constants is not included.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep score improvements honest by documenting remaining mismatch
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2492 reports a checkdiff improvement for ftCh_Init_80156AD8 but explicitly states the function is not a byte match and names the remaining register-allocation drift. This is a useful reporting pattern for partial matching improvements.
evidence_refs: pr_body: checkdiff -> 98.99317%
pr_body: Known remaining drift

accepted_candidate_records.title: Cached modifier mask in pad dispatch
accepted_candidate_records.text: In ftCh_Init_80156AD8, the PR replaced repeated L-button helper calls with a cached l_pressed mask while leaving other button checks as direct pad-word masks. Treat as a narrow Crazy Hand dispatcher matching tactic, not a global style rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: l_pressed introduction and L branch rewrites
pr_body: same style as matched Master Hand dispatcher

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate whether PR #2492 was superseded by another landed Crazy Hand dispatcher change
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2492 was closed unmerged and a commenter said 'Already done by me'. Curator may want to link this PR to the later or parallel landed change if one exists.
evidence_refs: metadata_json: merged_at null
text_corpus: jellejurre issue comment Already done by me
source_update_candidates.reason: Avoid treating this unmerged PR as the accepted source of the dispatcher cleanup if another PR landed equivalent work.

source_update_candidates.title: Check ownership for ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060
source_update_candidates.text: The PR declares and uses ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060 as float constants for zero and two, but the slice does not include symbol metadata or section ownership proof. If these symbols are real data-owned constants, data sources could record that ownership separately.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: extern f32 ftCh_Init_804DA05C and ftCh_Init_804DA060
pr_body: naming the zero/two float constants
source_update_candidates.reason: Current standards require data ownership evidence before promoting literals into named storage; this PR slice alone is insufficient.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the buttons macro as a global standard; it is a local tactic in an unmerged PR and the function remained unmatched.
Do not promote replacing float literals 0 and 2 with named externs as a general lesson; loaded standards caution against literal-to-symbol changes without data ownership evidence.
Do not infer reviewer intent or merge acceptance from the 'Already done by me' comment; it only supports that someone claimed equivalent work existed elsewhere.
